name: Contreras Pass Ascent — Utiel Loop
name_original: Subida al puerto de Contreras — Circular desde Utiel
description: This moderate 43.3-mile road cycling route from Utiel climbs to Contreras pass, offering panoramic views of vineyards and the reservoir.
region_intro: Road cycling around Utiel offers diverse landscapes, characterized by extensive vineyards, rolling hills, and sections of mountainous terrain. The region features mostly paved surfaces, with routes often traversing the La Plana de Utiel-Requena area. Natural features include the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park and river valleys like the Magro and Turia, providing varied backdrops for cycling. This area is known for its viticulture, with many routes winding through wine country. Best road cycling routes around Utiel The most popular road cycling route is Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant climb and offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Utiel to Tuejar via the Benageber Reservoir, a difficult 57.5 miles (92.5 km) path. This challenging route includes substantial elevation gain and passes by the Benageber Reservoir, offering scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Tour of Tierra Bobal — Circular from Utiel, a 48.1 miles (77.4 km) trail leading through the renowned Tierra Bobal wine region, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Utiel is defined by vast vineyards, mountainous terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Utiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Utiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Utiel?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Utiel, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, providing valuable insights.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Utiel?
A: Road cycling routes in Utiel feature diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by extensive vineyards and rolling hills. You'll also find sections of mountainous terrain, particularly in areas like the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. Most surfaces are paved, offering smooth rides through the La Plana de Utiel-Requena area.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Utiel?
A: Yes, Utiel offers a few easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Utiel?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Utiel has several difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853379536/from-utiel-to-tuejar-via-the-benageber-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Utiel to Tuejar via the Benageber Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a 57.5-mile (92.5 km) path that includes significant climbing and passes by the scenic Benageber Reservoir.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Utiel?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Utiel are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839859352/climb-to-contreras-pass-circular-from-utiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.3-mile (69.7 km) loop offering panoramic views. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858795899/tour-of-tierra-bobal-circular-from-utiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Tierra Bobal — Circular from Utiel&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the renowned wine region.

Q: Which routes offer the most scenic views in Utiel?
A: Many routes in Utiel offer beautiful scenery, particularly those winding through the vast vineyards of the La Plana de Utiel-Requena area. Routes that venture towards natural parks like Chera-Sot de Chera or pass by the Benageber Reservoir also provide stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839859352/climb-to-contreras-pass-circular-from-utiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic views.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the famous wine region?
A: Absolutely! Utiel is synonymous with wine, and many road cycling routes traverse its famous viticulture landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858795899/tour-of-tierra-bobal-circular-from-utiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Tierra Bobal — Circular from Utiel&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you through the heart of this renowned wine region, offering picturesque views of vineyards.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling near Utiel?
A: While cycling near Utiel, you can encounter several natural attractions. The Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park is a key highlight. You might also find routes that pass near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorros de Barchel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, especially during the rainy season, or the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Garita&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the cycling routes in Utiel?
A: Yes, Utiel's rich history offers several cultural points of interest. Cyclists can incorporate visits to sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4795711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, a 15th-century structure, or explore the historic center of Utiel with its unique medieval underground wineries. The region's wine-making heritage is evident throughout the landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Utiel?
A: The road cycling routes in Utiel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from vast vineyards to rolling hills and mountainous sections, as well as the mostly paved surfaces. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a frequently appreciated aspect.

Q: Is there a route that connects Utiel to other towns or natural areas?
A: Yes, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villargordo del Cabriel – Cementos Turia loop from Utiel&lt;/a&gt; offer connections to other towns and showcase the broader regional landscape. The 'Camino Natural Turia Cabriel' also provides a cycling-friendly path from Valencia to Utiel, with sections alongside the Magro River.

Q: Are there any routes that feature significant climbs or passes?
A: For those who enjoy climbing, Utiel offers routes with significant ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839859352/climb-to-contreras-pass-circular-from-utiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, featuring a notable climb and rewarding cyclists with expansive views from the pass.
tips: Ascent by road with very nice views throughout the journey. When you reach the viewpoint, the views are even more impressive, you can see the Contreras Reservoir and the Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel with their striking shapes.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel route, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts in Utiel. Its starting point is conveniently accessible by public transport. For those arriving by car, parking is generally available within Utiel, though specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, general town parking is usually sufficient for a circular route.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for all fitness levels?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces, it includes a significant climb with an elevation gain of approximately 755 meters over 69.7 kilometers. Riders should have good fitness to comfortably complete the ascent.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring mostly well-paved roads suitable for racebikes. You'll cycle through extensive vineyards, rolling hills, and sections of mountainous terrain. The route traverses the La Plana de Utiel-Requena area and follows river valleys like the Magro and Turia.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Riders can expect panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. A notable highlight is the impressive vista of the View of Embalse de Contreras and Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel, particularly from viewpoints along the ascent. The region's viticulture also means you'll pass through picturesque wine country.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures, though the region typically has many sunny days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially since it's in Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation.

faqs.question: Is the Climb to Contreras pass — Circular from Utiel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, and while dogs are generally allowed in natural parks, bringing a dog on a racebike route of this distance and difficulty is not practical or recommended. It's best suited for human cyclists only.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as De Juan Vich a Casas del Río, Camí Natural Turia - Cabriel, and Sendero Urbano turistico Utiel, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural areas and vineyards, specific cafes or water stops directly on the trail are not guaranteed. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's length and elevation gain. Utiel, the starting point, offers amenities.

faqs.question: What makes this route the most popular road cycling option in the Utiel area?
faqs.answer: Its popularity stems from a combination of factors: it offers a significant yet manageable challenge with its climb, features well-maintained roads, and provides breathtaking views of both natural landscapes like the Contreras Reservoir and agricultural scenery such as the extensive vineyards. Its circular nature and accessibility from Utiel also contribute to its appeal.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike route, you should bring your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water (at least 2-3 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a fully charged phone. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also advisable.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse de Contreras and Cuchillares de las Hoces del Cabriel
